subject: eol trades with glencore commodities limited

emails.subject: EOL Trades with Glencore Commodities Limited
emails.subject_base: EOL Trades with Glencore Commodities Limited
emails.body: Justin, Per the email below, before we respond to Glencore we just wanted to make sure that you haven't made any agreements with Glencore with respect to choice of law for the online trades that would subject them to UK law. I don't want to talk to them without checking with you first since this is one of the true counterparties that we are trading with on both sides of the Atlantic. By the way, I heard you had some really good news. I'm so happy for you! Forwarded by Tana Jones/HOU/ECT on 05/29/2001 01:53 PM Diane Anderson@ENRON 05/29/2001 11:29 AM

emails.subject: RE: EOL Trades with Glencore Commodities Limited
emails.subject_base: EOL Trades with Glencore Commodities Limited
emails.body: Thanks Justy, maybe you can just fax me a copy of your PA or ETA, so I can have a copy of the document when I speak to them. My fax no. is 713/646-3490. Thanks! Justin Boyd/Enron@EUEnronXGate 05/30/2001 04:32 AM

emails.subject: RE: EOL Trades with Glencore Commodities Limited
emails.subject_base: EOL Trades with Glencore Commodities Limited
emails.body: RE: EOL Trades with Glencore Commodities Limited Hi Tana Thanks for the note.....! I am looking for the original letter we signed with Glencore, but from my recollection, all we said to Gencore was that the choice of law under any master or other trading agreement between Enron and Glencore was unaffected by the choice of English law under the UK PA or ETA. This of course is not quite the same as Glencore's view below! J
